html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 孔氏志怪 - Title of the text, meaning 'Strange Tales of the Kong Family.' *志怪* refers to a genre of Chinese literature documenting strange or supernatural events.
  • 楚文王好田 - *好田* means 'enjoys hunting' or 'loves hunting.' Sets the context for the story, indicating that King Wen of Chu is a lover of hunting.
  • 天下快狗名鹰毕聚焉 - *天下快狗名鹰* refers to the finest dogs and eagles from across the land. *毕聚焉* means 'all gathered here,' indicating that the best hunting animals have assembled in Chu.
  • 俄而云际有一物凝翔 - *俄而* means 'soon' or 'suddenly.' *云际* refers to 'the clouds' or 'the sky.' *凝翔* means 'hovering' or 'gliding.' Describes a sudden appearance of a large bird in the sky.
  • 飘飖鲜白 - *飘飖* means 'fluttering' or 'swaying.' *鲜白* means 'bright white.' Describes the bird's appearance as bright and white.
  • 竦翮而升 - *竦翮* means 'raising its wings.' *而升* means 'and ascending.' Describes the eagle's action of rising into the air.
  • 矗若飞电 - *矗若* means 'as if it were.' *飞电* means 'a flying bolt of lightning.' Vivid metaphor comparing the eagle's speed to lightning.
  • 羽堕如雪 - *羽堕* means 'feathers falling.' *如雪* means 'like snow.' Describes the eagle's feathers falling in large quantities, like snow.
  • 血洒如雨 - *血洒* means 'blood spattering.' *如雨* means 'like rain.' Describes the eagle's blood spattering down like rain.
  • 良久 - Means 'after a long time' or 'after a while.'
  • 喙边有黄 - *喙边* means 'at the beak.' *有黄* means 'yellow.' Describes the bird's beak as having a yellow color.
  • 博物君子曰 - *博物君子* refers to a learned or knowledgeable person. *曰* means 'says.' Introduces a learned person's explanation of the event.
  • 此大鹏雏也，始飞焉，故为鹰所制 - *此大鹏雏也* means 'this is a young great peng.' *始飞焉* means 'just beginning to fly.' *故为鹰所制* means 'therefore, it was defeated by the eagle.' Explains that the large bird was a young great peng, which was still learning to fly, and thus was defeated by the eagle.
  • 乃厚赏献者 - *乃* means 'then' or 'therefore.' *厚赏* means 'greatly rewarded.' *献者* means 'the person who presented it.' Indicates that King Wen rewarded the person who brought the eagle.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 楚文王 - King Wen of Chu, a ruler of the Chu state during the Spring and Autumn period. Known for his military campaigns and his love of hunting.
  • 大鹏 - The *Da Peng* (Great Peng) is a mythical bird from Chinese mythology, often associated with the *Zhuangzi* (庄子), a Daoist text. The Great Peng is said to be the largest bird in the world, capable of flying across vast distances.
  • 初学记三十 - *初学记* is a collection of historical and literary anecdotes compiled during the Tang dynasty. The number '三十' (thirty) likely refers to the volume or section in which this story appears.

Proper Nouns

  • 楚文王 - King Wen of Chu.
  • 初学记 - *Chuxue Ji*, a collection of historical and literary anecdotes compiled during the Tang dynasty.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 举弓而射 - Means to lift the bow and shoot, describing an action.
  • 即中之 - Means 'hit it immediately,' referring to the target being hit.
  • 獐倒而复起 - Describes the animal falling down and then getting up again.
  • 不觉远 - Means 'without realizing how far,' indicating distance traveled unconsciously.
  • 忽见一里门如府舍 - Describes seeing a gate resembling a government building.
  • 中一铃，下有唱家 - Indicates a bell and a place where announcements are made.
  • 前充问曰 - Means 'Lu Chong went forward and asked.'
  • 此何府也 - Asks 'What kind of office is this?'
  • 我衣恶 - Means 'My clothes are shabby.'
  • 那得见贵人 - Means 'How can I see important people?'
  • 即有人提襆新衣迎之 - Means 'Someone immediately brought new clothes to welcome him.'
  • 充着，尽可体 - Means 'Lu Chong put them on, and they fit perfectly.'
  • 便进见少府展姓名 - Means 'He then entered to introduce himself to the lesser official.'
  • 酒炙数行 - Means 'They had several rounds of wine and food.'
  • 崔曰 - Means 'Cui said.'
  • 近得尊府君书 - Means 'Recently, I received a letter from your esteemed father.'
  • 为君索小女婚 - Means 'He sought marriage for his daughter.'
  • 故相延耳 - Means 'So I invited you here.'
  • 即举书示充 - Means 'He then showed the letter to Lu Chong.'
  • 充父亡时虽小 - Means 'Lu Chong's father had died when he was young.'
  • 然已见父手迹 - Means 'But he had seen his father's handwriting.'
  • 歔叹无辞 - Means 'He sighed and could not speak.'
  • 崔即敕内 - Means 'Cui then ordered the inner chambers.'
  • 令女郎庄严 - Means 'To have the lady prepare herself.'
  • 使充就东廊 - Means 'To send Lu Chong to the eastern corridor.'
  • 草堂诗笺二十七节引东廊作东厢 - Indicates a specific reference in a commentary.
  • 女已下车立 - Means 'The girl had already gotten off the carriage and stood there.'
  • 席头共拜焉 - Means 'They bowed together at the head of the seat.'
  • 三日毕 - Means 'After three days.'
  • 还见崔 - Means 'He returned to see Cui.'
  • 君可归矣 - Means 'You can return now.'
  • 女有娠相 - Means 'The girl has a pregnant appearance.'
  • 生男，当以相还 - Means 'If she gives birth to a boy, he will be returned.'
  • 生女，当自留养 - Means 'If she gives birth to a girl, she will be kept.'
  • 敕外严车送客 - Means 'He ordered the outer chambers to prepare the carriage to send the guest away.'
  • 崔送至门 - Means 'Cui saw him off to the door.'
  • 执手涕零 - Describes a poignant moment of farewell.
  • 离别之感，无异生人 - Means 'The feeling of separation was no different from that of living people.'
  • 复致衣一袭，被褥一副 - Means 'He also gave him a set of clothes and bedding.'
  • 充便上车去，如电逝 - Means 'Lu Chong then got into the carriage and left as fast as lightning.'
  • 须臾至家 - Means 'Soon he arrived home.'
  • 家人相见悲喜 - Means 'The family met and were both sad and happy.'
  • 推问 - Means 'They asked questions.'
  • 知崔是亡人 - Means 'They learned that Cui was a deceased person.'
  • 而入其墓 - Means 'And entered his tomb.'
  • 追以懊丧 - Describes a state of great distress or regret.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 卢充 - A name of a historical figure.
  • 范阳 - A historical region in China.
  • 崔少府 - A title indicating a lower-ranking official.
  • 府舍 - Refers to a government building or residence.
  • 少府 - A title for a lower-ranking official.
  • 里门 - Refers to a gate or entrance.
  • 唱家 - A place where announcements are made.
  • 东廊 - Refers to an eastern corridor, often used in traditional architecture.
  • 草堂诗笺 - A commentary on poetry.
  • 女郎 - Refers to a young woman or lady.
  • 庄严 - Means to prepare oneself, often in a formal or ceremonial context.
  • 东厢 - Another term for an eastern corridor.
  • 席头 - Refers to the head of a seat or table.
  • 三日 - Refers to a period of three days.
  • 敕 - An imperial decree or order.
  • 严车 - Refers to a prepared carriage.
  • 执手涕零 - Describes a poignant moment of farewell.
  • 生人 - Refers to living people.
  • 袭 - Refers to a set of clothes.
  • 被褥 - Refers to bedding.
  • 电逝 - Describes something moving very quickly, like lightning.
  • 须臾 - Refers to a short period of time.
  • 追以懊丧 - Describes a state of great distress or regret.

Proper Nouns

  • 卢充 - A historical figure.
  • 范阳 - A historical region.
  • 崔少府 - A title for a lower-ranking official.
  • 东廊 - A specific architectural feature.
  • 东厢 - Another term for an eastern corridor.
  • 草堂诗笺 - A commentary on poetry.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 居四年 - Temporal expression meaning 'after four years'.
  • 临水戏水中 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'playing in the water by the water'.
  • 乍浮乍没 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'rising and sinking alternately'.
  • 乍没作乍沉类林并同 - Parenthetical note indicating that the *Lei Lin* (类林) also uses 'sinking' instead of 'drowning'.
  • 欣然 - Idiomatic expression meaning 'with great joy'.
  • 欲握其手 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'wanted to take her hand'.
  • 举手指后车曰 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'pointed to the rear cart and said'.
  • 府君见人 - Phrase meaning 'the governor has seen me'.
  • 即见少府 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'then saw the Lesser Official'.
  • 充往问讯 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Chong went to greet him'.
  • 抱儿还充 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'handed the child back to Chong'.
  • 并赠诗曰 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'and gave a poem saying'.
  • 煌煌灵芝质，光丽何猗猗 - Poetic phrase meaning 'The lustrous quality of the lingzhi (灵芝), how splendid and graceful it is!'
  • 华艳当时显，嘉异表神奇 - Poetic phrase meaning 'Its brilliance was evident at the time, extraordinary and showing its miraculous nature'.
  • 含英未及秀，中夏罹霜萎 - Poetic phrase meaning 'It contained its essence but did not yet show its beauty; in midsummer, it withered under frost'.
  • 荣曜长幽灭，世路永无施 - Poetic phrase meaning 'Its glory and brightness will long be hidden, never to be used in this world'.
  • 不悟阴阳运，哲人忽来仪 - Poetic phrase meaning 'Not understanding the workings of yin and yang, a wise person suddenly appeared'.
  • 会浅别离速，皆由灵与祗 - Poetic phrase meaning 'Our meeting was brief, and our separation was swift, all due to the spirits'.
  • 何以赠余亲，金?可颐儿 - Poetic phrase meaning 'What can I give as a gift to you, a golden ornament for your child?'
  • 爱恩从此别，断绝伤肝脾 - Poetic phrase meaning 'Love and affection will be cut off from here, causing pain to the heart and spleen'.
  • 充取儿?及诗 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Chong took the child and the poem'.
  • 忽不见二车处 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'Suddenly, the two carts disappeared'.
  • 将儿还 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'carried the child back'.
  • 四座谓是鬼魅，佥遥唾之，形如故 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'The four people present thought it was a ghost or demon; all spat at it from a distance, but its form remained unchanged'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蒙求注 - Classical text known for its annotations on the *Mengqiu* (蒙求), a book of historical anecdotes.
  • 诗笺 - Classical text known for its annotations on the *Shijing* (诗经), the Book of Songs.
  • 类林 - Classical text known for its categorization of various subjects.
  • 崔氏 - Reference to the Cui family, a prominent and influential family in Chinese history.
  • 少府 - Official title, referring to a high-ranking government official.
  • 灵芝 - Mythical fungus believed to have healing properties and symbolizing longevity and immortality.
  • 阴阳运 - Refers to the balance and interplay of yin and yang, fundamental concepts in Chinese philosophy.
  • 哲人 - Term for a wise person or sage.
  • 灵与祗 - Refers to spirits or deities, often used in classical texts to explain supernatural phenomena.
  • 金? - Type of golden ornament, possibly a pendant or brooch.
  • 肝脾 - Organs associated with emotions and health in traditional Chinese medicine.

Proper Nouns

  • 蒙求注 - Classical text known for its annotations on the *Mengqiu* (蒙求).
  • 诗笺 - Classical text known for its annotations on the *Shijing* (诗经).
  • 类林 - Classical text known for its categorization of various subjects.
  • 崔氏 - Reference to the Cui family.
  • 少府 - Official title.
  • 灵芝 - Mythical fungus.
  • 金? - Type of golden ornament.
  • 肝脾 - Organs associated with emotions and health in traditional Chinese medicine.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 誰是汝父 - Direct question meaning 'Who is your father?' used to challenge the child's identity.
  • 傳省其詩 - Phrase meaning 'passed around and examined the poem,' suggesting a communal and careful reading.
  • 慨然嘆死生之玄通也 - Phrase meaning 'deeply sighed at the profound connection between life and death,' expressing a philosophical reflection.
  • 高舉其價，不欲速售 - Phrase meaning 'set a high price, not wanting to sell quickly,' indicating a deliberate and strategic approach to selling.
  • 冀有識者 - Phrase meaning 'hoping for someone who recognizes its value,' expressing a desire for recognition.
  • 欻有一老婢問充得?之由 - Phrase meaning 'suddenly an old maid asked Zong about the origin of the loom,' indicating an unexpected inquiry.
  • 還報其大家 - Phrase meaning 'reported back to her mistress,' indicating a chain of communication.
  • 大家，即女姨也 - Phrase meaning 'the mistress was the girl's aunt,' clarifying the relationship.
  • 遣視之，果是 - Phrase meaning 'sent to see it, and it was indeed so,' confirming the identity.
  • 我姨?崔少府女 - Phrase meaning 'my aunt's daughter, the daughter of Cui Shao Fu,' clarifying the lineage.
  • 未嫁而亡 - Phrase meaning 'died unmarried,' indicating a tragic fate.
  • 今視卿?甚似 - Phrase meaning 'now seeing your loom, it looks very similar,' indicating a visual similarity.
  • 得?本末，可得聞不 - Phrase meaning 'can you tell me the story of the loom?' expressing a request for more information.
  • 充以事對 - Phrase meaning 'Zong replied with the story,' indicating a detailed response.
  • 即字溫休 - Phrase meaning 'she was named Wenxiu,' indicating the naming of the child.
  • 蓋幽婚也 - Phrase meaning 'it was a secret marriage,' indicating a hidden or private union.
  • 其兆先彰矣 - Phrase meaning 'the signs were already evident,' indicating a premonition or foreshadowing.
  • 兒遂成為令器 - Phrase meaning 'the child grew up to be a capable person,' indicating success and achievement.
  • 其後生植，為漢尚書 - Phrase meaning 'later gave birth to Zhi, who became a Han imperial censor,' indicating the continuation of the family's success.
  • 植子毓，為魏司空 - Phrase meaning 'Zhi's son, Yu, became a Wei Grand Works Minister,' indicating further success.
  • 冠蓋相承至今也 - Phrase meaning 'the family has continued to be prominent until today,' indicating a lasting legacy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 崔少府 - Refers to Cui Shao Fu, a local official in the Han dynasty.
  • 二千石 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the Han dynasty, typically the governor of a commandery.
  • 尚書 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the Han and Wei dynasties, responsible for various administrative duties.
  • 司空 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the Han and Wei dynasties, responsible for public works and construction.
  • 幽婚 - Refers to a secret or private marriage, often involving supernatural elements.

Proper Nouns

  • 崔少府 - Cui Shao Fu, a local official in the Han dynasty.
  • 二千石 - A high-ranking official in the Han dynasty, typically the governor of a commandery.
  • 尚書 - A high-ranking official in the Han and Wei dynasties, responsible for various administrative duties.
  • 司空 - A high-ranking official in the Han and Wei dynasties, responsible for public works and construction.
  • 溫休 - The name given to the child, Wenxiu.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 敕其子曰 - Commanded his son to say; indicates a formal instruction.
  • 枪即化为清酒 - The spear immediately turned into clear wine; describes a magical transformation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 后汉末 - The end of the Eastern Han Dynasty; a period of political turmoil and the rise of warlords.
  • 华佗 - A famous physician in Chinese history; known for his medical skills and legendary cures.
  • 钟会 - A prominent figure in the Three Kingdoms period; known for his intelligence and military prowess.
  • 荀济北 - Likely a reference to Xun Jibei; a high-ranking official during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 孔氏志怪 - A collection of strange stories attributed to Kong; likely a classical text.

Proper Nouns

  • 华佗 - Hua Tuo; a famous physician.
  • 钟会 - Zhong Hui; a prominent figure in the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 荀济北 - Xun Jibei; a high-ranking official during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 孔氏志怪 - *Kong's Record of Strange Things*; a classical text.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 義興有邪足初學記引作白額虎，溪渚長橋有蒼蛟，並大啖人，並郭西周，時謂郡中三害。周即處也。 - A set phrase describing three major dangers in the county: a tiger with a white forehead, a green dragon, and Zhou (likely a person or place).
  • 經十年而母喪，開墓，其婢伏棺上。 - After ten years, the mother died, and the maid lay on the coffin.
  • 就視猶暖，漸有氣息；輿還家，終日而蘇。 - Looked at and still warm, gradually regained breath; carried back home, woke up after a day.
  • 說“寶父常致飲食，與之接寢，恩情如生。” - Said that Baobao's father often brought food and slept with her, their affection was like that of life.
  • 家中吉凶輒語之，校之悉驗。 - Told her about good or bad news at home, checked and found it to be true.
  • 平復數年後方卒。 - Recovered after several years and then died.
  • 寶因作搜神記，中云有所感起是也。 - Baobao then wrote *Search for the Supernatural*, in which he said that this was due to some kind of inspiration.
  • 晉明帝時，獻馬者夢河神請之，及至，帝夢同，即投河以奉神。 - During the reign of Emperor Ming of Jin, the horse presenter dreamed that the river god asked for the horse; when it arrived, the emperor had the same dream, and immediately threw the horse into the river to offer it to the god.
  • 始太傅褚亦好此馬，帝曰：“已與河神。”及褚公卒，軍人見公乘此馬矣。 - Originally, Grand Tutor Chu also liked this horse; the emperor said, 'It has already been given to the river god.' When Grand Tutor Chu died, soldiers saw him riding this horse.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 義興 - A place name, likely referring to a region in ancient China.
  • 邪足初學記 - A historical text.
  • 蒼蛟 - A green dragon, often associated with water and danger.
  • 干寶 - A famous historian and writer during the Eastern Jin dynasty, known for his work *Search for the Supernatural*.
  • 晉明帝 - Emperor Ming of Jin, a ruler during the Western Jin dynasty.
  • 太傅褚 - Grand Tutor Chu, a high-ranking official during the Jin dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 義興 - A place name.
  • 邪足初學記 - A historical text.
  • 蒼蛟 - A green dragon.
  • 干寶 - A famous historian and writer.
  • 晉明帝 - Emperor Ming of Jin.
  • 太傅褚 - Grand Tutor Chu.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 會稽盛逸 - A proper name referring to a person from the region of Kuaiji (modern-day Shaoxing, Zhejiang).
  • 晨興 - Literally 'early rise,' meaning to wake up in the morning.
  • 路未有行人 - Describes the scene where there are no people on the road.
  • 衣朱衣冠冕 - Refers to wearing red clothing, a hat, and a crown, indicating a formal or ceremonial appearance.
  • 俯以舌餂 - Means 'to lean down and lick with the tongue.'
  • 良久 - Indicates a long period of time.
  • 遽即隱不見 - Means 'suddenly disappeared.'
  • 會稽吏謝宗 - Refers to a local official named Xie Zong from Kuaiji.
  • 赴假吳中 - Means 'went on leave to Wu' (modern-day Suzhou, Jiangsu).
  • 獨在船 - Indicates being alone in a boat.
  • 姿性妖婉 - Describes a woman with a charming and seductive appearance.
  • 寄載 - Means 'to request a ride.'
  • 夕但聞言笑兼芬馥氣 - Describes hearing only voices and smelling a fragrant scent at night.
  • 邪魅 - Refers to a malevolent spirit or demon.
  • 蟲落 - Refers to a specific type of insect or spirit, possibly a local mythological creature.
  • 落民 - Refers to people associated with '蟲落.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 會稽 - An ancient name for the region of modern-day Shaoxing, Zhejiang.
  • 吳中 - Refers to the area around modern-day Suzhou, Jiangsu.
  • 朱衣冠冕 - Red clothing, a hat, and a crown, which were part of formal attire in ancient China.
  • 邪魅 - Malevolent spirits or demons, common in Chinese folklore.
  • 蟲落 - A specific type of insect or spirit, possibly a local mythological creature.
  • 落民 - People associated with '蟲落,' possibly a specific group or community.

Proper Nouns

  • 會稽 - The region of modern-day Shaoxing, Zhejiang.
  • 吳中 - The area around modern-day Suzhou, Jiangsu.
  • 盛逸 - A person from Kuaiji.
  • 謝宗 - A local official from Kuaiji.
  • 虫落 - A specific type of insect or spirit.
  • 落民 - People associated with '虫落.'
